title: Life expectancy, including UN projections
subtitle: The [period life expectancy](#dod:period-life-expectancy) at birth. This includes the observed life expectancy since 1950, and the medium-variant projections for the future, based on estimates by the UN Population Division.
caption:
Life expectancy, including UN projections

The period life expectancy at birth. This includes the observed life expectancy since 1950, and the medium-variant projections for the future, based on estimates by the UN Population Division.

The chart displays life expectancy trends from 1950 to 2100 for Japan, Sweden, Brazil, India, and the World average. Solid lines represent observed data from 1950 to approximately 2020, while dotted lines indicate future projections. In 1950, Japan starts near 60 years and rises to over 80 years by 2020, projecting toward 95 years by 2100. Sweden begins around 70 years, reaches the low 80s by 2020, and is projected to approach 90 years. Brazil and India start lower, near 45 and 40 years respectively in 1950, and both climb steeply to the high 60s or low 70s by 2020; their projections show continued growth, with Brazil nearing 85 years and India reaching roughly 80 years by the end of the century. The World average line starts near 48 years in 1950, rises to approximately 70 years by 2020, and projects to just over 80 years by 2100. A distinct dip is visible in the India series around 2020, dropping from roughly 70 to below 70 years before resuming its upward trajectory. Throughout the observed period, all series show an upward trend, with the gap between the highest and lowest values narrowing over time as the lower-starting countries experience faster rates of increase. By 2100, the projected values for all entities cluster between 80 and 95 years.
